cookies.text: This site uses only technically necessary cookies. For analytics (Vercel Analytics) we need your consent. More in the

hero.subline: Psychologically grounded 1:1 coaching for men between 22 and 38 who deliver on the outside — and don't arrive on the inside.
hero.imageAlt: Daniel Hanke in a Berlin street setting

problem.headline: You do everything right. And something is still missing.

cards.title: You're looking for validation — and don't notice it.
cards.body: When success comes, you feel good for a moment. When it doesn't, you collapse inside. And you compensate with more performance.

cards.title: You say yes when you mean no.
cards.body: You adapt, avoid conflict, make compromises that leave you hollow by evening. You betray yourself without noticing.

cards.title: You don't know what you actually want.
cards.body: Career, relationship, where to live — you wait for the moment of clarity that never comes. Because you never learned to listen to yourself.

approach.body1: Most men's coaches work on behavior. More discipline. More routines. Better pickup lines. That works for two weeks. Then you fall back — and you believe you've failed.
approach.body2: My approach: we work on the psychological structure underneath. Why do you do what you do? Why do you avoid what you avoid? What are you protecting without noticing?
approach.body3: The result isn't a 'new you.' It's a clearer one. One that decides from itself — not from the fear of being rejected.
approach.imageAlt: Daniel Hanke working at his desk

video.teaser: Clear, psychologically grounded videos. No hype, no script chasing clicks.

book.title: Selbstdisziplin 2.0
book.body: Discipline isn't a character trait. It's a system. In my book I break down why most self-discipline strategies fail — and which psychological levers actually hold. (Currently German only.)

newsletter.headline: What doesn't fit on YouTube.
newsletter.body: 1–2× per month. Longer pieces, more personal observations, tools I haven't published yet. No pitch, no funnel trick. If you ever want out: one click, you're gone.
newsletter.fineprint: By subscribing you agree to the mailing and the privacy policy.

outro.headline: If you feel like someone is finally holding up a mirror — write to me.
outro.subtext: Free. 45 minutes. Via Zoom. Not a sales call.

hero.headline: Working on the structure, not the surface.
hero.subline: Psychologically grounded 1:1 coaching via Zoom. For men who've had enough of motivational formulas and 5-step plans.

items.title: You write to me.
items.body: You answer the five questions below and send them to mail@danielhanke.com. If I believe I can help you, I'll get back to you within 48 hours.

items.title: We talk for 45 minutes — free, via Zoom.
items.body: No pitch. No script. You talk, I listen, I ask. At the end I'll tell you honestly whether coaching makes sense for you — and if not, I'll tell you that too.

items.title: We decide together.
items.body: If both sides want to, we set up the first sessions. One hour 90 €, paid in advance by bank transfer. No minimum term, no packages. We work as long as it makes sense.

items.title: We work — every 1–2 weeks, 60 minutes, via Zoom.
items.body: Between sessions: concrete exercises, brief check-ins by email if you get stuck. Confidential, no notes shared with third parties, no distribution.

benefits.eyebrow: What you get out of it

items.title: You understand why you do what you do.
items.body: We work on the patterns that drive your behavior. Once you see them, you stop sabotaging yourself — not through discipline, but through clarity.

items.title: You decide from yourself.
items.body: Career, relationship, where to live — you stop waiting for the right moment. Because you've learned to trust yourself.

items.title: You become genuinely present in relationships.
items.body: With women, friends, family. You stop protecting yourself, and you actually show up. That changes who responds to you — and how.

items.title: You finally feel like a man.
items.body: Not loud, not hard, not performative. Grounded. Reliable. Clear. Yourself — even when no one's watching.

fit.eyebrow: For whom — and for whom not
forYou: You're between 22 and 38 and live in the German-speaking region.
You function in life — and still feel like something is off.
You've read books, taken courses, tried discipline — and noticed: the problem is deeper.
You're ready to give honest answers, even when they're uncomfortable.
You understand that change takes time — no 30-day challenge.
notForYou: You're in acute psychological crisis (depression, anxiety disorder, trauma, suicidal thoughts). Then you need therapy, not coaching. I'm happy to help you find someone.
You're looking for pickup tricks or 'alpha frames'.
You expect me to tell you what to do.
You're not ready to invest in yourself — financially and time-wise.

request.headline: Write me an email. Answer these five questions.
request.body: No scheduling software, no online form. An honest email filters better than any tool. Answer as briefly or as fully as it feels right — one line per question is enough, more is fine too.

questions.title: What's going on in your life right now?
What have you tried so far to change it?
What would change concretely if we really moved something in the next 3 months?
Why now?
questions.body: What changed that you're writing today — and not in six months?

questions.title: Availability:
questions.body: Which weekdays and times work for a 45-minute first call (free, Zoom)?

request.subtext: I respond within 48 hours — even if the answer is 'no match'.

items.q: How many sessions do I need?
items.a: It depends on what you want to change. Some men work with me for 6 months, others for two years. There's no minimum term. We decide together after session 4 whether it fits.

items.q: What if I need therapy instead of coaching?
items.a: Then I'll tell you that honestly in the first call. Coaching works on patterns in the here and now. Therapy works on trauma, diagnoses, clinical symptoms. Sometimes you need both.
Yes. We talk every 1–2 weeks — but pauses are fine. I don't work with pressure.

items.q: Why not shorter sessions or groups?
items.a: 60 minutes 1:1 is the smallest meaningful unit. Group coaching is coming later (see Community).

community.headline: We're building.
community.body: A curated community for men who work seriously on themselves — with weekly live calls, tools, protected exchange. Launch: estimated 2026.
community.newsletterCta: Until then: subscribe to the newsletter
community.fineprint: By signing up you agree to the privacy policy.

hero.title: Selbstdisziplin 2.0.
hero.subline: Why most self-discipline strategies fail — and which psychological levers actually hold. Currently available in German only; an English edition is under consideration.

content.headline: Discipline isn't a character trait. It's a system.
content.body1: Most books on discipline sell you the same lie: be harder on yourself, get up earlier, endure more. That works for two weeks — until your system collapses under the pressure.
content.body2: Selbstdisziplin 2.0 breaks down why this approach fails. And shows how discipline actually arises: from inner clarity, smart structures, and psychological self-knowledge. Not from fighting yourself.
content.body3: It's not a motivation book. It's a toolbox. For men who understand: anyone who wants to deliver long-term has to stop working against themselves.

forWhom.headline: Who the book is for
items: You've read 'Atomic Habits' and noticed: there's still a layer missing.
You recognize yourself in discipline strategies that collapse after 30 days.
You want to understand what's behind discipline — not just how to perform it.

hero.title: Daniel Hanke.
hero.subline: Psychological counselor. Author. Coach for men who function in life — and not for themselves.

items: Born 1992 in Karlsruhe, currently lives in Leipzig — visually and culturally at home in Berlin.
M.A. Business Psychology and International Management, University of Heidelberg.
NLP Master Practitioner. Certified psychological counselor.
Author of 'Selbstdisziplin 2.0' and several other books.
Managing director of Next Level Education GmbH.

intro.title: How it started.
intro.body1: I was one of those boys. Overprotective mother, absent and choleric father, learned early to play the role that pleases others. At 14 I fell in love at a confirmation with a girl — she flirted with me, I ran away crying. That was the point I understood: something about me doesn't work.
intro.body2: Three years later I secretly attended an NLP seminar in Berlin, against my parents' wishes. It was the first time I acted against their expectation. It didn't 'heal' me. But it opened a door.

detour.title: The long detour.
detour.body1: Acting school in Kiel — dropped out. Teacher training in Tübingen — dropped out. Business psychology in Heidelberg — the best years of my studies. Master in International Management — a burnout I didn't admit for a long time.
detour.body2: After that: my own attempts at self-employment (Einserfabrik, books, a YouTube channel that drifted for years). Street fundraising for Greenpeace. Solar sales at Enpal in Berlin. Six months of sales training that taught me more than ten books. Travel — Thailand, a startup community in Malaysia. Then back.
detour.body3: It wasn't a straight path. It was a path with many wrong decisions — and I tell this not out of shame, but because I believe: anyone who claims to have done everything right is either lying or didn't risk enough.

why.title: Why coaching.
why.body1: At some point it became clear to me: I'd spent my whole life trying to repair something that couldn't be repaired. Only understood. And only what I'd understood, I could let go of.
why.body2: Today I work with men who stand in exactly that place. Who do everything they're supposed to — and don't arrive. I don't give them new tricks. I give them a mirror they can't hold up to themselves. And a sparring partner who doesn't look away when it gets uncomfortable.

distinction.headline: What you get from me — and what you don't.
get: Honest confrontation when needed — always with respect.
Concrete tools that work between sessions.

outro.headline: If you feel like you're talking to someone who understands you — write to me.

hero.title: What doesn't fit on YouTube.
hero.subline: 1–2 emails per month. Longer pieces, more personal observations, tools — that I don't share publicly.

expectations.headline: What to expect when you sign up

items.title: Deeper writing than on YouTube.
items.body: Some topics are too nuanced for a video. In the newsletter I have room to actually work them out.

items.title: Personal observations.
items.body: What I'm learning from coaching conversations (anonymized). What I'm reading, thinking, questioning.

items.title: Tools I don't publish anywhere else.
items.body: Sometimes exercises. Sometimes frameworks. Always: concrete, not abstract.

form.fineprint: By subscribing you agree to the mailing and the {privacyLink}. You can unsubscribe with one click anytime.

items.title: No funnel pitch.
items.body: I'm not selling you a coaching every third email.

items.title: No 'limited offer'.
items.body: If you want a coaching, you know where to find me.
